ORDER CLARIFYING INJUNCTION PLAINTIIF MUST DEPOSIT $4000 INTO PLAINTIFF'S TRUST ACCOUNT PRIOR TO THE COMMENCEMENT OF REPAIRS. PLAINTIFF STIPULATES THAT REMOVAL AND PAYMENT OF SAID FUNDS SHALL BE THE DICRETION OF THE COURT. DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE ACCESS TO PREMISES IN ORDER TO INSPECT AND TAKE PHOTOGRAPHS. DEFENDANT SHALL UTILIZE PRPOER SAFETY GEAR WHEN ENTERING THE SUBJECT PREMISES. PLAINTFF SHALL ADVISE DEFENDATN OF THE SCHEDULE OF REPAIRS AND WHEN ALLLS IN THE BATHROOM ARE TO BE REMOVED. DEFENDADNTS SHALL PROVIDE 24 HOUR NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF PRIOR TO INSPECTIONS. EFENDANT SHALL HAVE TWO (2) OPPORTUNITIES TO CONDUCT AN INSPECTION DURING THE COURSE OF REPAIRS. DEFEDANTS SHALL HAVE THE SUBJECT READY FOR PLAINTIFF TO COMENCE REPAIRS AND FREE OF HUMAN OCCUPANCY NO LATER THAN 9:00AM ON JULY 14, 2014

Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

No appearances.

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN PAXTON Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions is DENIED. Existing case law and the statutory language of Civil Code section 1717 objectively provided Plaintiff with enough support to make a good faith argument for extension of existing law in determining that Plaintiff is the prevailing party. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Garett L. Wong; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria L. Horcasitas, CSR No. 8683, Bus. Tel: 415-227-4060. (D501)

Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak (415-835-6777), counsel for defendant/opposing party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

Plaintiff'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees is DENIED. Moving party cites no authorities on point for the proposition that plaintiff is the prevailing party at this state og the litigation under the circumstances. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File A X-Complt
Off Calendar Sep-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

No appearances.

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN PAXTON's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. The delay in filing the cross-complaint is prejudicial. No evidence or legal authority is provided to support Defendant's contention that the cross-complaint is compulsory or that Plaintiff intends on dismissing the complaint. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: Order to Show Cause to tenant/Defendant is issued to be prepared by plaintiff's counsel to state that five days before the Order to Show Cause hearing, Defendant should file under oath the things that Defendant will agreed on repairs. Plaintiff's counsel will submit the Order To Show Cause by end of today's business date and to be served to the Defendant's counsel either by fax or email, by end of today's business date.
